title: Day 6 update: Trading bot Topstep challenge
author: Fun-Implement-8139
selftext:
Two Topstep Combine accounts running the same bot.

Account 1:

Monthly P/L: $825.82

. May 7: -$276.74

. May 11: +$32.26

. May 12: +$993.80

. May 13: -$181.24

. May 15: +$255.74 (2 trades)

Account 2:

Monthly P/L: $513.12

. May 7: -$278.74

. May 11: +$27.76

. May 12: +$993.80

. May 13: -$181.24

. May 14: +$382.76

. May 15: -$431.22

Honest disclaimer about today: both accounts took the same bot trade and ended in a loss. On account 1 I then took a manual trade myself to try and see how it would play out. That wasn’t the plan and it shouldn’t have happened. From now on I’m letting the bot do its thing without me interfering. I wanted to be upfront about it instead of hiding it.

Costs are not included in these numbers yet. I’ll break those down at the end of the month.

Quick note: this is still the Topstep Combine challenge, not a funded account yet. The P&L you see is simulated. I only start earning real money once I pass the challenge and get funded. The goal right now is to prove the bot can pass.

comments.author: grimmjoww1983
comments.body: Good going dude…how did you backtest your bot ?

replies.author: Fun-Implement-8139
replies.body: I bought a CSV file with 17 years of historical NQ futures data. 15-minute bars. Used that for the full backtest and the Monte Carlo simulations.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

replies.author: Fun-Implement-8139
replies.body: I attached the wrong pictures, reposted it again
